EisenhowerLogicSection.title: The Team Eisenhower Logic
EisenhowerLogicSection.doFirstDescription: Handle work that is both urgent and important straight away.
EisenhowerLogicSection.scheduleDescription: Plan work that is important but not urgent.
EisenhowerLogicSection.delegateDescription: Assign urgent but less important work to the right teammate.
EisenhowerLogicSection.eliminateDescription: Remove work that is neither urgent nor important.

HomeFooter.title: Move to the page you need quickly
HomeFooter.description: Browse the product overview, jump to key features, join a team, or head straight to your personal settings page.

AboutPage.title: I expanded the Eisenhower Matrix I used personally into a team conversation
AboutPage.description: This project started from real team meetings about priority. Every priority had a different reason behind it, and a team could not realistically work while looking only at the single most important task. That led me to extend the Eisenhower Matrix I used for myself into a team-level collaboration flow.

originCard.label: Where it started
originCard.title: The team needed a way to see the reasons behind priority together
originCard.description: A simple top-sorted list made it hard to explain why a task mattered now, or why it was urgent. This product is an attempt to reduce that friction.

AboutPage.storyDescription: The product started from scenes that kept repeating in real meetings. This page carries that problem statement directly.

meeting.title: Priority never came from a single reason
meeting.description: In meetings, some work had to move first because it was urgent, some because it was important, and some because it blocked other people. Priority was never well described by one score.

sorting.title: Traditional sorting felt limiting
sorting.description: A list sorted from top to bottom is convenient, but it does not help a team agree on why something belongs there. Tasks with very different reasons all collapse into the same rank.

expansion.title: I wanted to turn a personal tool into a team tool
expansion.description: What I liked about the Eisenhower Matrix I used personally was that it separated decision criteria instead of flattening them. That made me want to expand the same thinking into a shared workspace for teams.

AboutPage.principlesTitle: So this is how I am building it
AboutPage.principlesDescription: The focus is less on being a generic task list and more on giving teams a structure for explaining and adjusting priority together.

balance.title: Move beyond looking only at the top priority
balance.description: In real team operations, important work, urgent work, blocking work, and work that should be reduced all exist at the same time. The matrix is there to make those differences visible at a glance.

reason.title: Show decision criteria before rank order
reason.description: Once work is reduced to a list order, the reasoning behind priority disappears. This project tries to make both the placement and the reason behind that placement readable.

alignment.title: Turn personal productivity into team alignment
alignment.description: Rather than copying a personal productivity app as-is, I wanted to extend it into a collaborative experience where a team can look together, adjust together, and carry a weekly rhythm forward.

AboutPage.invitationTitle: I want this to feel like an idea born from real life
AboutPage.invitationDescription: Ideally, someone reading this page should feel that the product was made by a person who actually ran into this problem. At the same time, I want the page to leave room for the product to keep improving.
AboutPage.invitationCardTitle: The next features should come from real team voices
AboutPage.invitationCardDescription: Because of that, the project is less a finished answer and more an ongoing attempt to support better team priority conversations.

invitationPrompts.one: What would help a team capture the reason behind a priority more naturally?
invitationPrompts.two: What interaction would make it easier to agree on sorting criteria quickly?
invitationPrompts.three: What could connect this week's execution with last week's context more smoothly?

AboutPage.invitationFooter: I want this page to feel like the start of better feature suggestions and feedback.
